recipe.title: Bloomin' brilliant brownies
recipe.description: This easy brownie recipe is totally foolproof, and packed with dark chocolate, chopped nuts, cocoa powder and (optional!) sour cherries. Perfect for weekend baking.

steps.instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C/350°F/gas 4. Line a 24cm square baking tin with greaseproof paper.

on_enter.say: Preheat the oven to 180°C/350°F/gas 4. Line a 24cm square baking tin with greaseproof paper.

steps.descr: Snap the chocolate into a large bowl, add the butter and...
steps.instructions: Snap the chocolate into a large bowl, add the butter and place over a pan of simmering water, until melted, stirring regularly. Stir through the cherries and nuts (if using).

on_enter.say: Snap the chocolate into a large bowl, add the butter and place over a pan of simmering water, until melted, stirring regularly. Stir through the cherries and nuts (if using).

steps.descr: Sift the cocoa powder and flour into a separate bowl,...
steps.instructions: Sift the cocoa powder and flour into a separate bowl, add the baking powder and sugar, then mix together.

on_enter.say: Sift the cocoa powder and flour into a separate bowl, add the baking powder and sugar, then mix together.

steps.descr: Add the dry ingredients to the chocolate, cherry and nut...
steps.instructions: Add the dry ingredients to the chocolate, cherry and nut mixture and stir together well. Beat the eggs, then mix in until you have a silky consistency.

on_enter.say: Add the dry ingredients to the chocolate, cherry and nut mixture and stir together well. Beat the eggs, then mix in until you have a silky consistency.

steps.descr: Pour the brownie mix into the baking tin, and place in...
steps.instructions: Pour the brownie mix into the baking tin, and place in the oven for around 25 minutes. You don’t want to overcook them so, unlike cakes, you don’t want a skewer to come out clean – the brownies should be slightly springy on the outside but still gooey in the middle.

on_enter.say: Pour the brownie mix into the baking tin, and place in the oven for around 25 minutes. You don’t want to overcook them so, unlike cakes, you don’t want a skewer to come out clean – the brownies should be slightly springy on the outside but still gooey in the middle.

steps.descr: Allow to cool in the tray, then carefully transfer to a...
steps.instructions: Allow to cool in the tray, then carefully transfer to a large chopping board and cut into chunky squares. Delicious served with a dollop of crème fraîche or yoghurt mixed with some orange zest.

on_enter.say: Allow to cool in the tray, then carefully transfer to a large chopping board and cut into chunky squares. Delicious served with a dollop of crème fraîche or yoghurt mixed with some orange zest.
